subject: Physics
title: Is Gravity an Illusion?
description: A fast breakdown of Is Gravity an Illusion? for builders.

hook.text: Some physicists believe gravity isn't fundamental—it might emerge from deeper physics.

buildup.text: Erik Verlinde proposed gravity emerges from information and entropy, like heat from molecules.

discovery.text: In this view, gravity is a statistical effect arising because the universe seeks higher entropy.

twist.text: If true, hunting for gravitons is like searching for a particle of temperature—pointless.

climax.text: The idea is controversial but testable. Some dark matter predictions differ from standard models.

punchline.text: Maybe gravity isn't real. Maybe it just looks that way.

quiz.question: What does Erik Verlinde's entropic gravity theory propose?
options: Gravity emerges from information and entropy
Gravity is caused by dark matter
